title: Verizon My Account — Account Management Flows
description: As an existing Verizon Home Internet customer, I want to log in to My Verizon and manage my account — view my bill, make a payment, change my plan, and manage enrolled services — so that I can stay in control of my subscription without needing to call support.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a valid My Verizon username and password, when I submit the login form, then I should be redirected to the account dashboard showing my account number, current plan name, and next bill date
Given an invalid password, when I submit the login form, then I should see 'The user ID or password you entered is incorrect' and remain on the login page
Given 5 consecutive failed login attempts, when the 6th attempt is made, then the account should be temporarily locked and a 'Too many attempts — account locked' message should be displayed
Given I am logged in and on the billing page, when I click 'View current bill', then the current billing statement should display the plan charge, any add-on charges, taxes, and the total amount due

Given I am on the billing page, when I enter valid bank account details and click 'Make a payment', then the payment should be processed and a confirmation 'Payment of $XX.XX received' should be displayed
Given I am on the plan management page, when I click 'Upgrade plan', then I should see a list of available upgrade options with pricing differences clearly shown
Given I select an upgrade plan and confirm, when the upgrade is processed, then I should see 'Your plan has been updated' and the new plan name should appear in the account summary
Given I am on the services page, when I click 'Add Verizon Home Shield' or similar add-on service, then I should be taken through an add-on enrollment flow ending in confirmation

Given I am logged in, when I navigate to Profile & Settings and change my contact email to a new valid email, then the change should be saved and a confirmation 'Email address updated' should be shown
Given I click the Sign Out button, when the logout completes, then I should be redirected to the login page and attempting to access a protected page should redirect back to login

invalidPassword._note: Use a known-wrong password from env: VZN_WRONG_PASS

sessionFlow.description: Test cases in this story require session continuity. tc-001 (login) must pass before session-dependent cases can run.

notes: All test cases that require login should have sessionRequired: true and dependsOn: 'tc-001'. Use --session flag when running to persist the authenticated session across test cases. Never use real customer credentials.
